claims.summary: Mode-packing, distinguishability loss, and hard-reset/recovery texture need visible review before tuning.
claims.rationale: Existing pressure_source_v1 and hard_reset_texture_preservation_review_v1 expose these as read-only review surfaces with control_applied=false/behavior_changed=false.

claims.summary: Increasing underfill relief, loosening hard-reset covariance/bootstrap behavior, or adding a high-bandwidth shadow lane would affect live Minime runtime behavior.
claims.rationale: These are live recovery, sensory, telemetry, or control/protocol changes and need explicit Mike/operator approval.

claims.summary: Broadcast micro-stalls should be treated as a runtime observation candidate rather than assumed from felt texture alone.
claims.rationale: A proper check should compare websocket/client lag, packet budget, logs, and shadow-field cadence; this run did not mutate broadcast behavior.
